goal: Predict bulk_modulus, density, tg for PSTR (*CC(c1ccccc1)*)
assumptions: atactic PS (aPS): backbone CH2-CHPh stereocenter randomized at build; DP>=30 averages tacticity fluctuations in Tg (Soldera2006)
exp anchors for this member (PS): Tg=373 K, RT density=1.05 g/cm3, K=3.5-5.5 GPa
PCFF Class II has full parameter coverage for sp3 backbone C/H and aromatic ca/ha types in styrene

uncertainties.name: pcff_ps_tg_transferability
uncertainties.note: No direct PCFF PS Tg validation paper exists (confidence=medium). PCFF Class II validated on PC (Tg 417 vs 422 K) and PMMA vinyl backbone (<10%), but PS aromatic-pendant vinyl is an analogy, not a direct measurement. MD Tg expected high (430-500 K raw, SimPoly 399+/-22 K); 600 K sweep top covers it. literature_anchor (a cited PCFF or COMPASS PS Tg) would convert medium->high before committing to the Tg verdict.

uncertainties.name: K_underestimate_below_entanglement_dp
uncertainties.note: DP=40 < DP@Me=160 (aPS Me=16,600 g/mol). K from sub-entanglement chains is screening-grade; Born/NVT (rate-free) avoids the strain-rate inflation that deform would add. Report MD K vs exp 3.5-5.5 GPa and flag the offset; a converged K needs DP>=160 in a dedicated run (out of 48h budget).

uncertainties.name: hardware_optimum
uncertainties.note: hardware_policy.values_are_benchmarked=false (calibration was a partial/contended sweep). by_forcefield[pcff] default kept; benchmark on a drained box would authoritatively confirm gpu1_mpi4 for a ~6.4k-atom cell.

evidence.claim: PCFF via EMC (Class II). Switched from TraPPE-UA 2026-06-11: UA representation ignores phenyl ring partial charges and pi-dihedral barriers that govern PS Tg (~373 K); no TraPPE-UA PS Tg validation exists in literature. PCFF Class II explicitly parameterizes aromatic C-H charges and cross-terms; validated for PC (Tang2022, PCFF Tg=417K vs exp 422K) and PMMA vinyl backbone (NkepsuMbitou2025, Class II within 10%). Soldera2006 (COMPASS/OPLS) confirms MD can reach PS Tg ~449-484 K raw, WLF-corrected ~373 K. No direct PCFF PS Tg paper found; confidence=medium until confirmed.

alternatives.rejected_because: United-atom collapses phenyl ring H and omits aromatic partial charges + pi-dihedral barriers that govern PS Tg; no TraPPE-UA PS Tg validation in literature. Was the default until 2026-06-11.

alternatives.note: Soldera2006 reached PS Tg 449-484 K raw (WLF->373 K) with COMPASS/OPLS — viable but PCFF Class II chosen for EMC-native Class II coverage and PolyJarvis validation history (PC, PMMA).

evidence.claim: RESP (HF/6-31G*) recommended for PS/PSTR: phenyl pi-system polarization means QM-derived charges capture aromatic C-H partial charges that PCFF templates only approximate. AM1-BCC is an acceptable fallback.

charge_method_cost: RESP is the most expensive option — it requires a one-time HF/6-31G* single-point on the styrene repeat unit (minutes-to-tens-of-minutes on the build host) plus ESP fitting, vs near-zero for FF-embedded/Gasteiger and seconds for AM1-BCC (semi-empirical). The cost is incurred ONCE per chemistry at build time and is fully amortized over the multi-ns equilibration + Tg sweep + Born runs (tens of GPU-hours), so it adds <1% to total run cost. For an aromatic-pendant backbone the accuracy gain in ring C-H partial charges (which set density and Tg) outweighs the negligible marginal QM cost; AM1-BCC remains the fallback if the QM step is unavailable or fails.

alternatives.note: acceptable fallback; faster (seconds, semi-empirical) but slightly less accurate ring charge distribution

alternatives.rejected_because: near-zero cost but only approximates aromatic C-H charges; rejected because the dominant cost is the MD trajectory, not the one-time charge derivation, so the cheaper method buys no meaningful runtime while degrading ring-charge accuracy

evidence.claim: PSTR carries aromatic ring pi-system partial charges from PCFF (aromatic C-H); long-range Coulomb cannot be truncated without biasing density/Tg. PPPM required. lj/cut is only acceptable for purely apolar PHYC/PDIE backbones.

computational_cost: PPPM adds an FFT-based kspace solve each step (the reason mpi>=4 is mandated in D-08 — mpi=1 starves kspace ~75x), making it materially more expensive per step than lj/cut, which drops kspace entirely. For this ~6,400-atom cell the kspace overhead is modest and well within the 48h/1-GPU budget. The cost is accepted because the aromatic C-H partial charges are non-negligible (|q| ~0.1-0.15 e on ring C/H): truncating their Coulomb at the 12 A cutoff would bias density and Tg by ~5-20% for a charged backbone, which is the very property the run measures. The accuracy gain therefore dominates the kspace cost; lj/cut's speed advantage is only realizable for genuinely apolar backbones (PHYC/PDIE) where it introduces no bias.

alternatives.rejected_because: cheaper per step (no FFT kspace solve) but drops aromatic partial-charge Coulomb; would bias density/Tg 5-20% for a charged backbone — the accuracy loss outweighs the speedup at this cell size

evidence.claim: DP=40 >= DP_min=30, the atactic-tacticity-averaging floor (Soldera2006: DP>=30 needed to average backbone stereocenter fluctuations in Tg). Tg is chain-length independent above DP>=30, so 40 is a safe screening DP for the 48h budget (~6,400 atoms, ~10h on 1 GPU).
Tg estimated/anchored to PS experimental value 373 K; sweep window 200-600 K brackets both the exp Tg and the expected high MD Tg (430-500 K raw).
DP=40 is below DP@Me=160 (aPS entanglement); acceptable for Tg (chain-length independent) but makes K screening-grade and is recorded as a non-dominant uncertainty.

alternatives.rejected_because: needed for accurate K but ~4x larger cell exceeds the 48h budget; Tg does not require it

evidence.claim: PS is glassy at 300 K (exp Tg=373 K > 300 K), so K is taken via Born matrix + NVT: rate-free, gives the full elastic tensor, and avoids the strain-rate inflation (1.5-2x) that deform introduces. is_glassy will be confirmed from measured Tg>300 K (thermal track runs first).

alternatives.note: fallback only if Born fails; overestimates K 20-50% from rate artifact, compounding the sub-entanglement DP underestimate

alternatives.rejected_because: rubbery-phase method; PS is glassy at 300 K

evidence.claim: FF family = pcff (all-atom Class II + PPPM). Estimated cell ~6,400 atoms (DP40 x nchain10 x 16 atoms/styrene monomer) is <10k -> 1 GPU per run (comm overhead/GPU underfill above that). mpi=4 mandatory: mpi=1 starves PPPM kspace ~75x. This equals by_forcefield[pcff] default {gpu, mpi4, gpu1}.

directional_probe (measured 2026-06-19 on 4x A800 / 32 cores) recommends gpu1_mpi4 at 17.985 ns/day for pcff at cell_atoms=3020. My ~6,400-atom estimate is ~2.1x that cell, just past the [0.5x,2x] adoption window, and values_are_benchmarked=false (partial/contended sweep). Per policy I keep the by_forcefield default (which coincides with the probe winner) rather than adopting the probe number, and schedule a hardware_benchmark probe.
User resources (mpi_ranks<=4, gpu_ids=0) are consistent with the chosen {1 GPU, mpi=4} config.

alternatives.rejected_because: slower than gpu1_mpi4 (17.985) on this host per probe
cell <10k atoms and no clean benchmark support; policy requires both

findings: D-01_ff: all 4 criteria covered; source_doi + 5 citations; PCFF covers styrene atom types; TraPPE/OPLS alternatives recorded — pass.
D-03_electrostatics: |q|>0.1e aromatic ring charges require pppm; satisfied with evidence and lj/cut alternative rejected — pass.
D-04_system_size: DP=40>=20 for Tg; sub-entanglement K limitation acknowledged as non-dominant uncertainty (not hidden) — pass.
D-07_property_method: glassy phase -> Born+NVT (rate-free), is_glassy from measured Tg>300K, deform fallback recorded — pass.

D-08_hardware: pin {gpu,1,mpi4} == by_forcefield[pcff] default; mpi>=4 for PPPM, 1 GPU for 6.4k-atom cell, concurrent mpi<=32 all satisfied; values_are_benchmarked=false handled correctly via scheduled hardware_benchmark probe + confidence:low — pass.

Stage schema: all 8 stages carry stage/track/success_criteria; tracks valid and match track_map; planned_stages cover all 3 requested properties; dominant uncertainty named with valid literature_anchor probe — pass.
